title: Melvin Sparks

[Soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"), [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop"), [jazz blues](/wiki/Jazz_blues "Jazz blues")

(March 22, 1946 – March 15, 2011)[[1]](#cite_note-All-1) was an American [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"), [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop") and [jazz blues](/wiki/Jazz_blues "Jazz blues") [guitarist](/wiki/Guitarist "Guitarist"). He recorded a number of albums for [Prestige Records](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), later recording for [Savant Records](/wiki/Savant_Records "Savant Records"). He appeared on several recordings with musicians including [Lou Donaldson](/wiki/Lou_Donaldson "Lou Donaldson"), [Sonny Stitt](/wiki/Sonny_Stitt "Sonny Stitt"), [Leon Spencer](/wiki/Leon_Spencer "Leon Spencer") and [Johnny Hammond Smith](/wiki/Johnny_Hammond_Smith "Johnny Hammond Smith").[[1]](#cite_note-All-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American jazz guitarist (1946–2011)

| Melvin Sparks | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | March 22, 1946  [Houston](/wiki/Houston "Houston"), [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). |
| Died | March 15, 2011(2011-03-15) (aged 64)  [Mount Vernon](/wiki/Mount_Vernon,_New_York "Mount Vernon, New York"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States |
| Genres | [Soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"), [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop"), [jazz blues](/wiki/Jazz_blues "Jazz blues") |
| Occupation | [Guitarist](/wiki/Guitarist "Guitarist") |
| Instrument | Guitar |
| Years active | 1960s–2011 |
| Labels | [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), [Savant](/wiki/Savant_Records "Savant Records") |

Musical artist

**Melvin Sparks** (March 22, 1946 – March 15, 2011)[[1]](#cite_note-All-1) was an American [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"), [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop") and [jazz blues](/wiki/Jazz_blues "Jazz blues") [guitarist](/wiki/Guitarist "Guitarist"). He recorded a number of albums for [Prestige Records](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), later recording for [Savant Records](/wiki/Savant_Records "Savant Records"). He appeared on several recordings with musicians including [Lou Donaldson](/wiki/Lou_Donaldson "Lou Donaldson"), [Sonny Stitt](/wiki/Sonny_Stitt "Sonny Stitt"), [Leon Spencer](/wiki/Leon_Spencer "Leon Spencer") and [Johnny Hammond Smith](/wiki/Johnny_Hammond_Smith "Johnny Hammond Smith").[[1]](#cite_note-All-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Melvin_Sparks&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Sparks was born in [Houston](/wiki/Houston "Houston"), [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), and raised in a musical family. He received his first guitar at age 11. Sparks began working in the [rhythm and blues](/wiki/Rhythm_and_blues "Rhythm and blues") genre as a high school student, first with [Hank Ballard](/wiki/Hank_Ballard "Hank Ballard") and [the Midnighters](/wiki/The_Midnighters "The Midnighters"), and then with [the Upsetters](/wiki/The_Upsetters_(American_band) "The Upsetters (American band)"), a touring band formed by [Little Richard](/wiki/Little_Richard "Little Richard"), which also backed [Jackie Wilson](/wiki/Jackie_Wilson "Jackie Wilson"), [Curtis Mayfield](/wiki/Curtis_Mayfield "Curtis Mayfield") and [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye").[[2]](#cite_note-chinen-2)

Sparks moved to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and worked as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") for [Blue Note](/wiki/Blue_Note_Records "Blue Note Records") and Prestige Records. As part of the burgeoning [soul-jazz](/wiki/Soul-jazz "Soul-jazz") scene of the late 1960s and early 1970s, Sparks often backed organists like [Jack McDuff](/wiki/Jack_McDuff "Jack McDuff"), [Dr. Lonnie Smith](/wiki/Dr._Lonnie_Smith "Dr. Lonnie Smith"), [Charles Earland](/wiki/Charles_Earland "Charles Earland") and [Leon Spencer](/wiki/Leon_Spencer "Leon Spencer"). Sparks released his debut album, *Sparks!*, for Prestige in 1970.[[2]](#cite_note-chinen-2)

He was seen on Northeastern television commercials as the voice of [Price Chopper](/wiki/Price_Chopper_(New_York) "Price Chopper (New York)")'s House of BBQ advertising campaign.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Sparks died on March 15, 2011, at age 64, at his home in [Mount Vernon, New York](/wiki/Mount_Vernon,_New_York "Mount Vernon, New York"). He had diabetes and high blood pressure.[[2]](#cite_note-chinen-2)

## Discography

### As leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Melvin_Sparks&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: As leader")]

* *[Sparks!](/wiki/Sparks! "Sparks!")* ([Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), 1970)
* *[Spark Plug](/wiki/Spark_Plug_(album) "Spark Plug (album)")* (Prestige, 1971)
* *[Akilah!](/wiki/Akilah! "Akilah!")* (Prestige, 1972)
* *Texas Twister* (Eastbound, 1973)
* *Melvin Sparks '75* ([Westbound](/wiki/Westbound_Records "Westbound Records")/20th Century, 1975)
* *I'm Funky Now* (Westbound/20th Century, 1976)
* *Sparkling* ([Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records"), 1982)
* *I'm a 'Gittar' Player* (Cannonball, 1997)
* *What You Hear Is What You Get* ([Savant](/wiki/Savant_Records "Savant Records"), 2003)
* *It Is What It Is* (Savant, 2004)
* *This Is It!* (Savant, 2005)
* *Groove On Up* (Savant, 2006)
* *Live at Nectar's* (One Note, 2017)
